dict.string:
𝐌𝐀𝐈𝐍 𝐅𝐄𝐀𝐓𝐔𝐑𝐄𝐒
❖ Add any of your favorite artists, albums, or playlists by copying and pasting their Spotify link. ❖ Randomly select one of your added artists/albums/playlists and shuffle play it. ❖ Works only for Spotify Premium Users. ———————————————————— 𝐅𝐔𝐋𝐋 𝐃𝐎𝐂𝐔𝐌𝐄𝐍𝐓𝐀𝐓𝐈𝐎𝐍
https://routinehub.co/shortcut/17835/ ———————————————————— 𝐂𝐑𝐄𝐃𝐈𝐓𝐒
❖ @Sn0w0d → The “Ultimate Spotify Shortcut” gave me some ideas of how to use the Spotify Web API in shortcut code. Unfortunately, that shortcut doesn’t work as intended anymore.
You must be connected to the internet to use this shortcut.

Looks like you are a new user! To start using this shortcut, you need to authorize the Spotify API to be able to play songs on your behalf.
After tapping 𝗢𝗞, a web view will pop up and you may be asked to log in to your Spotify account, followed up by asking for authorization to use the Spotify API.
After that, your authorization code will appear on the screen. Tap on 𝗖𝗼𝗽𝘆 𝘁𝗼 𝗖𝗹𝗶𝗽𝗯𝗼𝗮𝗿𝗱 to copy the authorization code, and tap on 𝗗𝗼𝗻𝗲 to exit the web view.
Oops...something went wrong. Please try again! If the issue still persists, please contact the developer of this shortcut.
You are all set up now! Spotify Shuffle will work as intended the next time you run this shortcut. Happy jamming! 🎶

Uh oh...seems like you don't have any of your favorite artists/albums/playlists added to this shortcut.
You can add artists/albums/playlists to this shortcut by editing this shortcut and scrolling to the 𝗦𝗲𝘁𝘁𝗶𝗻𝗴𝘀 section. The 2nd dictionary allows you to add the name and the Spotify link to your artist/album/playlist.
Oops...something went wrong. Please try again! If the issue still persists, please contact the developer of this shortcut.

Thanks for downloading 𝐒𝐩𝐨𝐭𝐢𝐟𝐲 𝐒𝐡𝐮𝐟𝐟𝐥𝐞! I hope you find this shortcut to be helpful! If you haven’t already, I recommend checking out the complete documentation of this shortcut wherever you downloaded it from. Before you can start using this shortcut, some settings must be filled in to ensure this shortcut is tailored to you. The next question(s) will ask you for your input on these settings. If you are a previous user of this shortcut, you can copy and paste the settings from the old shortcut to the new shortcut before deleting the old one. To learn more about each setting or how to transfer settings, please check out the 𝗦𝗲𝘁𝘁𝗶𝗻𝗴𝘀 section on the documentation page. As a friendly reminder, you can change/view these settings anytime by editing the shortcut and scrolling to the dictionaries under the 𝗦𝗲𝘁𝘁𝗶𝗻𝗴𝘀 section.
𝐒𝐏𝐎𝐓𝐈𝐅𝐘 𝐀𝐏𝐈 𝐒𝐄𝐓𝐓𝐈𝐍𝐆𝐒 You can learn more about each setting in the documentation page.

𝐌𝐔𝐒𝐈𝐂 𝐒𝐄𝐓𝐓𝐈𝐍𝐆𝐒 You can add links to any of your favorite artists, albums, or playlists here, for the shortcut to play. To add one, tap 𝗔𝗱𝗱 𝗻𝗲𝘄 𝗶𝘁𝗲𝗺, then 𝗧𝗲𝘅𝘁, and type the name of it (where it says 𝘒𝘦𝘺), and paste the Spotify link to it (where it says 𝘛𝘦𝘹𝘵). Don’t worry, you can add more later, too! An example playlist is added to show you the correct format. You may remove it, if you don’t want it in your list, by tapping ⛔️ and then 𝗗𝗲𝗹𝗲𝘁𝗲.
